subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 4
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: What is 4 × 6?
questions.explanation: 4 rows of 6 dots is 24.

questions.questionText: Which number is shown by the place value blocks?
questions.explanation: 2 hundreds, 5 tens, and 3 ones make 253.

questions.questionText: What is 47 rounded to the nearest ten?
questions.explanation: 47 is between 40 and 50. The ones digit is 7, so round up to 50.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 4 equal parts are shaded, so 3/4.

questions.questionText: A store had 326 apples. They got 158 more. How many apples are there now?
What is 500 − 264?
There are 28 crayons shared equally among 4 students. How many crayons does each student get?
questions.explanation: 28 ÷ 4 = 7 crayons each.

questions.questionText: What time is shown on the clock?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is between 2 and 3 and the minute hand is on 6, so it is 2:30.

questions.questionText: What is 7 × 5?
How many books did Ben read?
questions.explanation: Ben's bar reaches 8 books.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to 1/2?
questions.explanation: Bar B has 3 of 6 parts shaded, which matches 1/2.

questions.questionText: What is the area of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 7 × 3 = 21 square centimeters.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 6 + 4 + 6 + 4 = 20 cm.

questions.questionText: What number does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: Counting by 2s, the point sits on 14.

questions.questionText: A box holds 9 markers. How many markers are in 6 boxes?
questions.explanation: 6 × 9 = 54 markers.

questions.questionText: What is 56 ÷ 8?
Each 🍎 stands for 2 apples. How many apples were picked on Tuesday?
questions.explanation: Tuesday shows 5 apple symbols, and each stands for 2, so 5 × 2 = 10.

questions.questionText: What is 384 rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: 384 is closer to 400 than to 300, so it rounds to 400.

questions.questionText: A movie starts at 4:15 and lasts 45 minutes. What time does it end?
questions.explanation: 4:15 plus 45 minutes is 5:00.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is greater, 3/8 or 5/8?
questions.explanation: With the same denominator, 5/8 has more shaded parts than 3/8.

questions.questionText: What is the name of this shape?
questions.explanation: A shape with 6 sides is a hexagon.

questions.questionText: Which multiplication fact does this array show?
questions.explanation: There are 3 rows of 8 dots, which is 3 × 8 = 24.

questions.questionText: How many more votes did pizza get than tacos?
questions.explanation: Pizza had 12 votes and tacos had 7. 12 − 7 = 5.

questions.questionText: Which equation has the same answer as 8 + 8 + 8?
questions.explanation: 8 + 8 + 8 is three groups of 8, which equals 3 × 8 = 24.

questions.questionText: A pizza is cut into 6 equal slices. Maya eats 2 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.explanation: 6 − 2 = 4 slices are left, so 4/6 of the pizza remains.

questions.questionText: Liam buys 4 packs of pencils with 6 pencils in each pack. He gives away 9 pencils. How many pencils does he have left?
questions.explanation: 4 × 6 = 24 pencils, then 24 − 9 = 15.

questions.questionText: A baker made 48 muffins and put them equally into 6 boxes. Then she sold 3 boxes. How many muffins were sold?
questions.explanation: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 muffins per box. 3 boxes × 8 = 24 muffins sold.

questions.questionText: A rectangle has an area of 36 square feet. If one side is 4 feet long, how long is the other side?
questions.explanation: Area = side × side, so 36 ÷ 4 = 9 feet.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is the smallest?
questions.explanation: When the numerator is 1, the larger the denominator, the smaller the fraction. 1/6 is smallest.

questions.questionText: Recess started at 10:50 and ended at 11:25. How long was recess?
questions.explanation: From 10:50 to 11:00 is 10 minutes, plus 25 more to 11:25 is 35 minutes.

questions.questionText: Select all the multiplication facts that equal 24.
Select all the even numbers.
questions.explanation: 18 and 40 end in an even digit, so they are even. 27 and 53 are odd.

questions.questionText: Select all the fractions that are equal to 1 whole.
questions.explanation: When the top and bottom numbers are the same, the fraction equals 1. 2/3 is less than 1.

questions.questionText: Select all the shapes that are quadrilaterals (have exactly 4 sides).
questions.explanation: Squares, rectangles, and trapezoids all have 4 sides. A triangle has 3 sides.

questions.questionText: Select all the numbers that are greater than 365.
questions.explanation: 370 and 401 are greater than 365. 356 and 299 are less than 365.

questions.questionText: What is 8 × 4?
questions.explanation: 8 rows of 4 is 32.

questions.questionText: A library had 412 books. 175 were checked out. How many books are still on the shelf?
A square has sides of 8 cm. What is its perimeter in centimeters?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides: 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 = 32 cm.

questions.questionText: There are 7 tables with 5 chairs each, plus 4 extra chairs. How many chairs are there in all?
questions.explanation: 7 × 5 = 35 chairs, then 35 + 4 = 39.

questions.questionText: A whole pie is cut into 8 equal pieces. Write the fraction of the pie that is 3 pieces.
questions.explanation: 3 of the 8 equal pieces is 3/8 of the pie.
